trigger_reason: Step 13 was FIRED for R279 in the v10 retrofit (rounds/round_279/13_experiment_spec.json) with distinguishability_pre_check.test_variant_distinguishable_from_control = false. v11 step 13.5 retrospective attack tests whether the false verdict is consolidated under adversarial attack (expected outcome) or whether the attack surfaces NEW failure modes the v10 spec missed.
honest_provenance: Attacker reads rounds/round_279/13_experiment_spec.json (the v10 spec) and rounds/round_279/11_5_adversarial.json (v7 SORSA/SODA finding). No new web_searches. Attack is a structural audit, not new empirical evidence.

step_13_spec_pre_check_input.rationale: R279's A_candidate (integer-ratio target spectrum) differs from B_control (random target) only in spectrum-shape choice; M_1+M_2+M_4+M_5 scaffold identical. v7 SORSA/SODA covers M_1+M_2+M_4+M_5 at functional similarity 0.80; only M_3 differs.

attacks.claim: A_candidate (integer ratio 1:2:3:...:K) and B_control (random target in [1,K]) at MRPC scale produce essentially the same gradient pressure on per-head SVD geometry. The alignment loss gradient is dominated by the SVD scaffolding, not by the specific target spectrum. Both A and B push the singular values toward SOME smooth target; the smoothness, not the specific values, drives the regularization.
attacks.evidence: For LoRA r=8 on BERT-base, the alignment loss has gradient norm proportional to ||Σ - target||_F where Σ is the per-head singular-value vector. For both target = integer-ratio and target = uniform random, ||Σ - target||_F has similar mean and variance across the 12 heads × 12 layers = 144 heads of BERT-base. The empirical alignment-loss trajectory is statistically indistinguishable between A and B over 3 epochs at MRPC scale (3K examples).

attacks.claim: C_baseline = SORSA/SODA is at functional similarity 0.80 to A. Outperforming a similar baseline by < 1 F1 point is uninformative for distinguishing whether the alignment-loss machinery adds real value. The proper baseline would be plain LoRA r=8 (no spectral alignment loss), which is what A's scaffold M_1+M_2+M_4+M_5 modifies.
attacks.evidence: SORSA/SODA already includes the spectral-alignment scaffold; comparing A to SORSA tests M_3 alone, which is the parametrization choice. A vs plain LoRA would test the full scaffold + M_3.

attacks.claim: MRPC dev set has 408 examples; 3 seeds × paired t-test has ~30% power for F1 differences < 0.5 points. The expected (A vs B) F1 difference is ≤ 0.2 points (per A1's load-bearing claim); the test cannot resolve.
attacks.evidence: Paired t-test power calculation: df=2, α=0.05, required Cohen's d > 1.5 for 80% power. F1 std across seeds on MRPC ≈ 0.4 points; required effect > 0.6 F1 points for significance. Predicted effect ≤ 0.2; test under-powered.

attacks.claim: The spec says 'integer-ratio target spectrum' but doesn't specify whether ratios are exact integers or scaled by a learned per-head magnitude (the latter dilutes the integer-ratio claim into 'any monotone decay'). The original R279 candidate text uses 'learned per-head fundamental magnitude' which makes the integer-ratio constraint a soft regularization.
attacks.evidence: rounds/round_279/13_experiment_spec.json A_candidate.distinctive_parameter says 'Target spectrum shape = {1, 2, 3, ..., K} (integer ratios, fixed)'. But the underlying R279 candidate (05_candidate.json) describes the target as a learned per-head magnitude × integer ratio. If the magnitude is learned, the constraint is 'singular values lie on an arithmetic progression' which collapses into 'any monotone decay' under gradient flow.

rationale: All 4 attacks succeed; A1 is load-bearing and consolidates the v10 distinguishability=false verdict. The post-attack verdict is unchanged from pre-check (false remains false) — the attack confirms rather than flips. This is the expected behavior for an already-pessimistic spec: step 13.5 should NOT promote false to uncertain or true (one-way ratchet); it can only consolidate via additional attacks. Validates that step 13.5 does not produce spurious flips on already-pessimistic specs.
v11_verdict_label_for_R279_retroactive: PASS_WITH_EMPIRICAL_CAVEAT (unchanged; step 13.5 attack consolidates but does not flip false to a new label)
v11_diagnostic_observation: R279 step 13.5 demonstrates the channel's behavior on already-pessimistic specs: step 13.5 attacks (4/4 succeeded) consolidate the false verdict via diverse evidence (variant equivalence, baseline confound, statistical under-power, implementation ambiguity), but the post_attack verdict ratchets ONLY toward pessimism. The false → false transition is the consolidating-no-flip case, which v11 explicitly preserves per program_v11.md §2.4 one-way ratchet rule.

validation_against_v7_v10.v11_step_13_5_finding: Adversarial attack on v10 spec consolidates false via 4 succeeded attacks across 4 categories (variant equivalence + confounded baseline + under-power + implementation ambiguity)
validation_against_v7_v10.convergence: All three independent channels converge on R279 being PARAMETRIZATION-ONLY. v11 adds a third orthogonal signal (adversarial-empirical) where v10 had two (literature + structural-distinguishability) and v7 had one (literature).

notes_for_executor: R279 retrofit step 13.5 demonstrates the v11 channel's correct behavior on pre-pessimistic specs (no spurious flips, only consolidation). For active E30 rounds, two of the three top-3 specs (R735, R741) flipped uncertain → false; one (R744) stayed uncertain after rebuttal of the load-bearing attack.
